title: Kimi K2.5
oneLiner: Moonshot AI's open-weight multimodal model for agentic and tool-using workflows.
summary: Kimi K2.5 is Moonshot AI's powerful open-weight model line, positioned for multimodal and agentic workflows with API access and public model materials.
whyItMatters: Kimi K2.5 matters because it sits at the intersection of frontier-style agent behavior and open model distribution. For teams comparing model choices for coding, tool use, and multimodal tasks, it is one of the models worth evaluating closely.
bestFor: Teams comparing open-weight models for tool-using agents
Developers testing coding and reasoning workflows with Kimi-compatible APIs
Researchers watching how Chinese open model labs approach multimodal agents
notFor: Teams that require a pure OSI-style open-source license without extra model terms
Users who want a local-only consumer app with no API or model operations work
seoTitle: Kimi K2.5: Models resource for open AI builders
seoDescription: Kimi K2.5 profile on OpenAgent.bot: what it is, when to use it, official links, open-source status, and structured resource data.
shareDescription: Moonshot AI's open-weight multimodal model for agentic and tool-using workflows.

coreStrengths.title: Agentic model positioning
coreStrengths.description: Kimi K2.5 is marketed around tool use and agentic tasks rather than only chat.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: That makes it a serious candidate for OpenAgent-style workflows.

coreStrengths.title: Open-weight distribution
coreStrengths.description: Moonshot publishes public repository and model access materials for evaluation.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Open weights let builders compare behavior outside a single hosted interface.

coreStrengths.title: Multimodal direction
coreStrengths.description: The Kimi K2.5 line is positioned as a multimodal model rather than a text-only assistant.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Multimodal inputs are increasingly important for agents that read screens, documents, and visual context.

useCaseNotes.title: Agent model comparison
useCaseNotes.description: Evaluate Kimi K2.5 against Qwen, GLM, and DeepSeek-style models on tool-using workflows.

useCaseNotes.title: Coding and repository tasks
useCaseNotes.description: Test whether it can reason across code, instructions, and tool calls in realistic engineering loops.

useCaseNotes.title: Multimodal assistant prototypes
useCaseNotes.description: Use it as a candidate when an assistant needs more than plain text input.

compareNotes.title: Strong candidate for open-weight agent testing
compareNotes.summary: Kimi K2.5 is useful when the comparison target is agentic behavior and multimodal capability, but its modified license terms should be reviewed carefully.

commandLine.label: Clone the model repository
commandLine.description: Start with official model materials and license notes before using it in production.

faq.question: Is Kimi K2.5 open source?
